example_prompt: Fetch all Notch Pay beneficiaries with pagination support.

search.description: Optional search string to filter beneficiaries by name, email, or account number.

total.description: Total number of beneficiaries across all pages.

per_page.description: Number of items returned per page.

last_page.description: Last available page number. Use to detect end of pagination.

gotchas: The Authorization header value is your public key with no prefix — do not add 'Bearer ' before it.
Query parameters (limit, page, search) must be appended to the URL as a query string — they are not sent in the request body.
When current_page equals last_page, you have reached the end of the list. Requesting beyond last_page returns an empty items array rather than an error.
The total field reflects the count at the time of the request; concurrent creates or deletes can cause inconsistencies when paginating large lists.
This endpoint requires your Notch Pay **private key** in the Authorization header, not the public key used for create_payment. Using a public key returns 406 Not Acceptable.
